hero_subtitle: SFTY helps digital asset exchanges unify multi-jurisdictional compliance workflows under MiCA by connecting provider outputs, regulatory rules, and audit evidence into one operational layer.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Compliance gaps slow MiCA exchange readiness
problem_description: Exchanges struggle to align fragmented compliance controls with evolving MiCA requirements across jurisdictions. This creates delays in onboarding, inconsistent risk reviews and weak audit trails that challenge defensible regulatory reporting and operational oversight.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates jurisdiction-specific rules, provider outputs, onboarding data and case workflows to create one operational compliance layer that supports audit-ready MiCA alignment for exchanges.
workflow_title: From regulatory updates to internal rules
workflow_description: SFTY monitors jurisdiction-specific regulatory changes and maps them to internal compliance rules supporting timely updates, policy alignment and audit evidence for exchange teams.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects multiple provider outputs into coordinated workflows, decisions, cases and evidence to help exchanges manage MiCA readiness across jurisdictions efficiently.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports regulated exchanges with governed provider workflows, audit-ready compliance evidence and scalable deployment for multi-jurisdictional MiCA requirements from day one.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support MiCA compliance readiness workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how exchanges manage MiCA onboarding and screening
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence supports MiCA reporting requirements

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for MiCA compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY helps exchanges align MiCA compliance by connecting multi-jurisdictional rules, onboarding, screening, reporting workflows and evidence into one consistent, auditable compliance operating layer.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support Travel Rule workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY manages Travel Rule workflows by routing messages, tracking exceptions, linking originator and beneficiary data, creating cases for issues, and preserving relevant evidence for MiCA readiness.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit-ready evidence by securely logging decisions, case histories, provider outputs and regulatory updates to support review, compliance verification and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects existing KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into a connected workflow without disrupting current systems, enabling controlled integration across jurisdictions.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace Travel Rule or KYC providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but connects their outputs into structured workflows, decisions, cases and reports for clearer compliance management under MiCA.
final_title: Structure MiCA compliance workflows with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects provider outputs, regulatory rules and audit evidence into one controlled MiCA compliance workflow.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
